patch.value: Stale-lock takeover race in acquireLock: two waiters can both remove the stale lock and both believe they own the item

handleExistingLock (src/core/lock/lock.ts) is check-then-act: waiter A and waiter B both read the lock file, both classify it stale (TTL expired), then both unlink+create. Interleaving: A unlinks stale → A creates fresh lock (wx succeeds) → B (which read the OLD stale payload before A's create) unlinks A's FRESH lock → B creates its own → A and B hold the "lock" simultaneously and both mutate the item; the loser's writeFileAtomic is silently overwritten. Window is small (only opens when a stale lock is contended, e.g. after a crashed process, right when parallel agents pile on) but it is exactly the extreme case the lock exists for. Fix options: (a) after createLockFile succeeds following a stale takeover, read the lock back and verify pid+owner+created_at match our payload, else treat as lost and re-loop; (b) takeover-by-rename: rename the stale lock to a unique tombstone (rename fails for the second waiter → ENOENT → re-loop) before creating the fresh lock; (c) compare-and-unlink guard via fs.stat inode/mtime captured at read time. Same pattern exists in the retry loop's 3-attempt cap: with N>3 waiters on a stale lock, some callers exhaust attempts and get a generic 'Failed to acquire lock' — fold into the pm-2muu wait-budget loop when both land.
